steps.title: Support a net increase
steps.content: The researchers conclude that net CO<sub>2</sub> is likely to increase if warming causes earlier snow melt. A supporting finding must show early snow melt either reduces CO<sub>2</sub> absorption, increases CO<sub>2</sub> output, or both.

steps.title: Reject unclear or opposite effects
steps.content: Choice A increases plant absorption and leaves respiration unchanged. Choice B suppresses respiration, which would reduce output. Choice D mentions stronger solar-radiation effects but does not show the net direction. The finding that reduces plant growth and raises heterotrophic respiration directly supports more net CO2. The answer is C.
